hero.headline: Just Speak. VoxInk Writes.
hero.subtitle: AI voice input for Android / Windows / Linux. Bring your own API key. No subscription.

speed.title: Lightning Fast
speed.description: Groq Whisper speech recognition — results in 0.5 seconds, the world's fastest inference

refine.title: Smart Refinement
refine.description: AI removes filler words, fixes grammar, adds punctuation — speak and it's ready

multilang.title: Multilingual
multilang.description: Traditional Chinese, English, Japanese, and mixed-language support with auto-detection

transcribe.title: File Transcription
transcribe.description: Transcribe audio and video files to text, with SRT subtitle export

byok.title: Bring Your Own Key
byok.description: Use your own Groq / OpenAI key. No subscription. You control the cost.

zeroCollection.title: Zero Data Collection
zeroCollection.description: No account, no tracking, no telemetry. Completely anonymous usage.

directApi.title: Direct API Connection
directApi.description: Your voice goes straight to your chosen AI provider — never through VoxInk servers.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
steps: Sign up at groq.com for free and get an API key
Install VoxInk and paste your API key in settings
Enable VoxInk keyboard (Android) or press the hotkey (Desktop)

android.name: Android
android.description: Voice keyboard — use in any app

windows.name: Windows

footer.description: AI voice input — open source, privacy first

guide.title: Get Started in 5 Minutes
guide.subtitle: From Groq signup to voice input — a complete step-by-step guide

step1.title: Step 1: Create a Groq Account
step1.description: Go to groq.com and click "Sign Up" in the top right. Register with Google or email.

step2.title: Step 2: Get Your API Key
step2.description: After logging in to Groq Console, click "API Keys" in the left menu, then "Create API Key". Name it (e.g. "VoxInk"), create it, and copy the key — it's only shown once.

step3.title: Step 3: Install VoxInk
step3.android: Search for "VoxInk" on Google Play, or download the latest APK from GitHub Releases.
step3.windows: Download the latest Windows installer (.exe) from GitHub Releases and run the setup wizard.
step3.linux: Download the AppImage from GitHub Releases. Run chmod +x VoxInk*.AppImage, then double-click or run from terminal.

step4.title: Step 4: Configure Your API Key
step4.description: Open VoxInk settings, find the "Speech" section. Select Groq as the provider, paste your API key, and click "Test" to verify the connection.

step5.title: Step 5: Start Using
step5.android: Android: Go to Settings → Language & Input → Manage Keyboards → Enable "VoxInk Voice Keyboard". Switch to VoxInk in any app and tap the mic button.
step5.desktop: Desktop: Press the hotkey (default Ctrl+Shift+V) and start speaking. Release to auto-paste the transcription at your cursor.

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: Groq offers a generous free tier with daily API call and token limits. Normal daily usage (dozens of voice inputs) typically stays within the free tier. For heavy users, Groq offers affordable pay-as-you-go pricing.

q1.question: Forgot my API key?
q1.answer: Log in to Groq Console, delete the old key in the API Keys page, and create a new one. Paste it back into VoxInk settings.

q2.question: Inaccurate speech recognition?
q2.answer: Check your language setting ("Auto-detect" is recommended). Use in a quiet environment for best results. Enable "Smart Refinement" to let AI auto-correct recognition errors.

q3.question: Can I use OpenAI instead of Groq?
q3.answer: Yes. Switch the provider to OpenAI in VoxInk settings and paste your OpenAI API key. However, Groq is faster and has a free tier.

q4.question: Works offline?
q4.answer: VoxInk requires an internet connection for AI-powered speech recognition and refinement. Offline use is not currently supported.

privacyPage.title: Your Voice, Your Data
privacyPage.subtitle: How VoxInk's privacy-first architecture keeps your data safe

architecture.title: Privacy by Design
architecture.flow: Your Device → Direct to AI Provider → Text Response → Local Storage
architecture.noServer: No VoxInk server ever touches your data

byok.title: BYOK Model
byok.description: Bring Your Own Key — you get your own API key, and your voice data goes directly to your chosen AI provider (Groq, OpenAI, etc.). VoxInk never proxies, relays, or stores your data.

zeroCollection.title: Zero Collection Policy
zeroCollection.description: VoxInk requires no account registration, tracks no usage behavior, and sends no telemetry. No analytics tools, no crash reporters, no "anonymous usage statistics". What you use and what you say — only you know.

encryption.title: Local Encrypted Storage
encryption.description: API keys are protected by Android Keystore (Android) or Tauri encrypted store (Desktop) — never stored in plain text. Transcription history is in a local database that you can delete anytime.

concerns.title: Privacy Concerns with Voice Input Services
concerns.intro: Some subscription-based voice input services have these data flow risks:
risks: Your voice is first sent to the service provider's server, then relayed to the AI — an extra invisible intermediary
Account creation with payment info required — your identity and usage can be fully correlated
Usage behavior may be tracked for "service improvement" or "personalization"
Servers may temporarily cache or log your voice data, even when claiming otherwise

overview.title: Overview
overview.content: VoxInk (語墨) is an open-source AI voice keyboard that operates on a BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) model. We do not collect, store, or transmit any user data to our servers.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection
dataCollection.intro: VoxInk does not collect any personal data.
items: No analytics or telemetry
No crash reporting to our servers
No user accounts or registration
No advertising or tracking

audioData.title: Audio Data
audioData.intro: When you use VoxInk for voice input:
items: Audio is recorded locally on your device
Audio is sent directly from your device to the API provider you configured (e.g., Groq, OpenAI)
Audio is not stored on your device after transcription completes
VoxInk has no access to the audio data sent to your API provider
audioData.providerNote: Your API provider's privacy policy governs how they handle the audio data.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
items: API keys are stored locally on your device using Android EncryptedSharedPreferences (Android) or Tauri encrypted store (Desktop)
Keys are encrypted at rest using platform encryption mechanisms
Keys are never transmitted to any server other than the API provider you configured
Keys are never logged or displayed in full

transcriptionHistory.title: Transcription History
items: Transcription results are stored locally on your device in a local database
You can delete individual transcriptions or all data at any time
No transcription data leaves your device unless you explicitly share or export it

llmRefinement.title: LLM Refinement
items: Transcribed text is sent directly to your configured LLM provider (e.g., Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic)
VoxInk does not see or store the refined text beyond your local device
Your LLM provider's privacy policy applies to this data

permissions.title: Permissions
items: RECORD_AUDIO — Voice recording for speech-to-text
INTERNET — Sending audio to your API provider

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.content: VoxInk communicates only with the API providers you explicitly configure. We do not integrate any third-party SDKs for analytics, advertising, or tracking.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.content: VoxInk does not knowingly collect data from children under 13. Since VoxInk collects no data at all, this is inherently satisfied.

changes.title: Changes
changes.content: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Changes will be posted in the app's repository.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For privacy questions, please open an issue on our GitHub repository.

android.steps: Install from Play Store, or download APK and allow unknown sources.

windows.steps: Run the installer and follow the wizard. VoxInk appears in the system tray.

linux.steps: Run chmod +x VoxInk.AppImage, then double-click or run from terminal.
